title: Instructional Resources: Library Materials
description: Conroe ISD's guidelines for library materials explain how books and other resources are selected, maintained, and made available in school libraries. The policy also establishes a process for community members and parents to raise concerns about specific materials they believe may be inappropriate for students. Understanding these guidelines helps families know how library collections are managed and how they can participate if they have questions or objections about available materials.

timeline_local.summary: This is not a policy change document. It is a board agenda item requesting approval of library materials in accordance with Texas Education Code §33.026, Board Policy EFB, and Senate Bill 13. The Board of Trustees is required to consider and approve all library materials donated to or proposed for procurement in District school and classroom libraries. The Superintendent submits a certified list of recommendations, which is made available for public review for at least 30 days before the Board votes to approve or reject the list.

timeline_local.summary: This is not a redline policy document. It is an agenda item cover sheet for Conroe ISD requesting board approval of library materials in accordance with Texas Education Code §33.026, Board Policy EFB, and Senate Bill 13. The Board is required to approve all library materials donated to or procured for District school and classroom libraries. The Superintendent submits a certified list of recommended additions or removals; the list is publicly available for at least 30 days before the Board votes. Exemptions apply for replacement copies, additional copies of already-cataloged titles, or materials previously approved for the same grade levels.

## Item 8: Approval of Library Materials (Texas Education Code 33.026, Board Policy EFB, Senate Bill 13)

**Presenter:** Hedith Upshaw, with Mindy Harding (Assistant Director of Education Technology)

- September through November acquisitions list; thousands of books.
- All materials went through 30-day public access review.
- All books have appropriate ISBN numbers as required by state law.
- District attested all materials are in accordance with the recently adopted book policy
timeline_local.notes: This page (p. 82) is an agenda cover/recommendation sheet, not a redline policy document. No policy language additions or deletions are shown. The policy reference cited is EFB (Legal and Local). The action is board approval of a library materials list, not a change to the policy itself.

timeline_local.summary: Adds definitions and prohibitions related to pornographic/sexually explicit library and instructional materials, referencing S.B. 12 (89th Texas Legislature). Prohibits possession, acquisition, and purchase of pornographic library materials. Defines 'pornographic material' broadly to include harmful material under Penal Code 43.24, indecent/profane content, sexually explicit material under Education Code 33.021, and materials that refer students to pornographic websites via links or QR codes.
changes_added: Definition of 'pornographic material' including: 'Harmful material' as defined by Section 43.24, Penal Code; 'indecent content' that portrays sexual or excretory organs or activities in a way that is patently offensive; 'profane content' including grossly offensive language considered a public nuisance; library material rated sexually explicit by selling vendor; 'sexually explicit material' as defined by Education Code 33.021; library material containing indecent or profane content; any material that is pervasively vulgar or educationally unsuitable as referenced in Board of Education v. Pico; any material that refers a person to an Internet website containing pornographic material including by use of a link or QR code as defined by Section 443.001 of the Texas Health and Safety Code.
The possession, acquisition, and purchase of pornographic library materials is prohibited.
timeline_local.discussion: policy EM (teaching about controversial issues). He urged the board to give these revisions appropriate scrutiny.
timeline_local.notes: Plain text extraction; underline/strikethrough formatting not preserved. Changes inferred from context. Cross-referenced with EFB(LEGAL) and EMB(Local). Adoption inferred from triage hint.

description_local: This policy governs the selection, use, and removal of library and instructional materials in the district's schools, including new rules about what types of content are prohibited. It applies to all library books and teaching materials available to students, and establishes clear definitions around sexually explicit or pornographic content that cannot be included in school collections. Parents and community members should be aware of this policy because it directly shapes what reading and learning materials students can access at school, and it reflects recent state legislation aimed at setting stricter content standards for school libraries.
